description: Figure 10 presents bright-field TEM micrographs with inset selected-area diffraction patterns (SADP) along the [110] zone axis, revealing the deformation microstructures of γ-austenite in near-rapidly solidified Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.2C-xCr (x = 0, 3, 6 wt.%) steel strips after cold rolling with 20% reduction. Subfigures (a), (b), and (c) correspond to the 0Cr, 3Cr, and 6Cr alloys, respectively, all exhibiting {111} planar slip bands. The images visually demonstrate a systematic decrease in slip band spacing with increasing Cr content: the 0Cr specimen shows widely spaced slip bands, whereas the 3Cr and 6Cr specimens display progressively higher slip band densities. Caption-reported measurements indicate average spacings of 371 ± 47 nm, 100 ± 15 nm, and 45 ± 13 nm for 0Cr, 3Cr, and 6Cr, respectively. This Figure supports the core conclusion that decreasing slip band spacing with Cr addition is responsible for the enhanced reinforcement effect after cold rolling.
